text: The optimal taper duration for maximizing performance is 2 weeks, producing a large positive effect on performance.
Training volume should be exponentially decreased by 41-60% during a taper to maximize performance gains.
Training intensity should not be reduced during a taper; maintaining intensity produces significant positive effects on performance.
Training frequency should not be reduced during a taper; maintaining frequency produces significant positive effects on performance.
An exponential taper pattern is superior to other taper patterns (e.g., linear or step reduction) for optimizing performance.
During a taper, the primary training variable to manipulate is volume, while intensity and frequency should be maintained at pre-taper levels.
